# openai-agents-rust docs summary

Compact export of the markdown docs tree.

- README.md: Documentation
  The canonical docs home is [index.md](index.md).
- agents.md: Agents
  Use this page when you want to understand what an `Agent` is, how to build one, and which pieces belong on the agent versus on the runner.
- config.md: Configuration
  Use this page when you need to understand what is configured on `RunConfig`, what is configured on `RunOptions`, and how overrides should be layered.
- context.md: Context
  Use this page when your runs or tools need application state such as user identity, tenancy, feature flags, or service handles.
- examples.md: Examples
  Use this page when you want runnable starting points instead of longer conceptual guides.
- guardrails.md: Guardrails
  Use this page when you need runtime checks that can stop or rewrite unsafe inputs, outputs, or tool traffic.
- handoffs.md: Handoffs
  Use this page when you want control to move from one agent to another instead of staying inside one agent’s tool call.
- human_in_the_loop.md: Human In The Loop
  Use this page when a tool, shell action, MCP call, or workflow step should require operator review before it executes or before its result is accepted.
- index.md: openai-agents-rust docs
  This is the product docs home for the Rust agents runtime. Start here if you want to build with the library, understand the runtime model, or find the right subsystem to extend.
- mcp.md: MCP
  Use this page when you want to discover tools and resources from Model Context Protocol servers instead of baking every tool into the process.
- models/README.md: Models
  Use this section when you want to understand how model selection works, what the default OpenAI path does, and where model-level settings belong.
- models/openai.md: OpenAI Models
  Use this page when you want the OpenAI-specific runtime behavior: default provider resolution, hosted tools, Responses, Chat Completions, and conversation-aware sessions.
- models/providers.md: Model Providers
  Use this page when you need to decide where model resolution should live and how provider selection interacts with the runner.
- models/settings.md: Model Settings
  Use this page when you need to shape provider requests without hardcoding those choices inside the agent instructions.
- multi_agent.md: Multi-Agent Patterns
  Use this page when a single agent stops being enough and you need delegation, specialization, or routing.
- quickstart.md: Quickstart
  Use this page when you want the fastest path from an empty Rust project to a working agent run.
- realtime/README.md: Realtime
  Use this section when you need a long-lived session that can send and receive incremental events instead of waiting for one final result.
- realtime/audio.md: Realtime Audio
  Use this page when your realtime flow includes audio input, audio output, or playback state tracking.
- realtime/events.md: Realtime Events
  Use this page when you want to understand what flows through a realtime session as it runs.
- realtime/transports.md: Realtime Transports
  Use this page when you need to choose or understand the transport beneath a realtime session.
- ref/README.md: Curated Reference
  Use this section when you already know what you want to do and need the fastest map to the public surface.
- ref/openai.md: OpenAI Reference
  Use this page when you need the OpenAI-specific public surface without rereading the conceptual guides first.
- ref/realtime.md: Realtime Reference
  Use this page when you want the realtime session API map: runner, session, event, and transport-facing types.
- ref/runtime.md: Runtime Reference
  Use this page when you already know the concept you need and want the shortest path to the relevant runtime type or function.
- ref/voice.md: Voice Reference
  Use this page when you need the voice workflow and pipeline surface at a glance.
- release.md: Release
  Use this page when you are preparing a release or making a change that affects the public docs surface.
- repl.md: REPL
  Use this page when you want to poke the runtime interactively without building a whole application first.
- results.md: Results
  Use this page when you need to inspect what actually happened during a run, continue a conversation, or replay the output as the next input.
- running_agents.md: Running Agents
  Use this page when you want to choose the right execution entry point and understand how the runtime moves through turns.
- sessions/README.md: Sessions
  Use this section when you want state to survive across turns, runs, or process boundaries.
- sessions/memory.md: Memory And SQLite Sessions
  Use this page when you need a local session backend without OpenAI-specific conversation semantics.
- sessions/openai.md: OpenAI Sessions
  Use this page when you want sessions that preserve OpenAI conversation behavior rather than only storing local item history.
- streaming.md: Streaming
  Use this page when you need visibility into the run before the final output is ready.
- tools.md: Tools
  Use this page when you want the model to trigger typed behavior instead of only returning text.
- tracing.md: Tracing
  Use this page when you need to understand what happened during a run and you want that story in spans instead of only in logs.
- usage.md: Usage
  Use this page when you care about turns, usage, replay growth, and the operational shape of your agent runs.
- visualization.md: Visualization
  Use this page when you want diagrams or graph-shaped representations of an agent system.
- voice/README.md: Voice
  Use this section when you want STT -> workflow -> TTS orchestration on top of the shared agents runtime.
- voice/pipeline.md: Voice Pipeline
  Use this page when you need the full STT -> workflow -> TTS loop.
- voice/workflow.md: Voice Workflow
  Use this page when you want to understand how voice uses the shared runner rather than bypassing it.
